name: Loanmax Title Loans
description_short: Loanmax Title Loans Mesa, Arizona — LoanMax Title Loans provides quick cash loans secured by vehicle titles, with loans up to $10,000 and same-day fundi...
description_long:
LoanMax Title Loans was founded in 1990 in Jonesboro, Georgia, and has operated for over 25 years as a title loan provider across multiple states. The company positions itself as helping "hardworking Americans use the equity in their vehicle to access the cash they need" for short-term financial emergencies. The Las Vegas location at 3276 N. Las Vegas Blvd is one of five locations in the Las Vegas metro area, with additional offices in Henderson, Nevada.

The company offers auto title loans (also called car title loans) with loan amounts up to $10,000, subject to vehicle evaluation and ability to repay. The application process requires only a vehicle, photo ID, and a clear vehicle title. LoanMax accepts multiple payment methods including cash, check, debit card, and Western Union. The company operates during business hours Monday-Friday 10 AM–6 PM and Saturday 9 AM–2 PM (with some location variations). Customers can apply in person, call the store, or use online tools including online payment and account registration systems.

LoanMax distinguishes itself through its multi-state presence and 25+ year operating history, which suggests established regulatory compliance frameworks. The company provides state-specific consumer disclosures and licensing information for Virginia, Delaware, South Carolina, New Mexico, Nevada, and Michigan. For Nevada customers specifically, the company includes a regulatory warning that title loans "should be used for short-term financial needs only and not as a long-term financial solution," and recommends credit counseling for customers with credit difficulties. The company maintains a corporate complaint process and lists regulatory contact information for multiple states.

However, LoanMax is a title loan provider, which means customers risk losing their vehicle if they default on the loan. The website does not disclose APR rates, fees, or loan terms, making it difficult for consumers to evaluate the true cost before contacting the company. Nevada's regulatory disclosures indicate concern about predatory lending practices, and the company's lack of rate transparency on the public-facing website reflects the high-cost nature of title loans. This product is best suited for consumers with genuine short-term cash emergencies who have no other options and fully understand the vehicle-loss risk.
pros: Fast funding available with same-day or next-day processing for qualified applicants
Loan amounts up to $10,000, larger than typical payday loans
Multiple convenient Las Vegas-area locations (5 in Las Vegas/Henderson metro)
Minimal documentation required (vehicle, photo ID, clear title only)
Multiple payment methods accepted (cash, check, debit card, Western Union)
Established company with 25+ years of operating history since 1990
Online payment and account management tools available for convenience
cons: No APR, interest rates, or fee information disclosed on website—requires in-person or phone inquiry
Risk of vehicle repossession if loan payments are missed or defaulted
Intended only for short-term needs; Nevada regulators warn against using as long-term solution
High-cost loan product with rates typically well above 36% APR based on industry standards
Company's own regulatory disclosures recommend credit counseling before applying, indicating lender recognizes this is a last-resort product
best_for: Consumers with immediate cash emergencies and a vehicle with clear title who have exhausted other options
Borrowers who need more than $1,000 but cannot qualify for personal loans or credit-based products
Short-term bridge financing needs (1-3 months) where vehicle risk is acceptable
diagnosis: LoanMax Title Loans is best for consumers facing genuine short-term cash emergencies who own a vehicle with equity and have no access to cheaper credit. The critical caveat is that title loans carry high costs (rates not disclosed on website but typically 100%+ APR), pose immediate risk of vehicle loss, and should never be used as recurring or long-term financing—warnings the company itself emphasizes in Nevada regulatory disclosures.
services: In-person loan applications at physical locations
FAQ and loan benefit information resources
